warnings: cultural-validator WARNING (2 warn-severity checks; pre-existing repo drift)

objective: Phase 1 of CODEREF-CORE pipeline refactor. Ratify canonical IDs, add file-grain taxonomy fields, collapse the parallel scanner, and remove overclaimed feature claims.

handoff.reason: Phase 1 implementation belongs to the project agent. Orchestrator authored the plan; CODEREF-CORE executes.

execution_rules: Phase 1 hard-stops before Phase 2. Do not begin relationship-fact split or header parser implementation in this workorder.
STANDARDS/layers.json is the only source of truth for the layer enum. Do not duplicate the enum as a TypeScript literal union.
Every emitted ElementData must carry headerStatus (default 'missing' until the parser ships in Phase 2.5).
Phase 0 ground-truth tests must continue to fail for the same reasons. Any new failure or any unexpected pass is a regression and must be reported.
Only one project-wide AST walk per populate-coderef --semantic invocation. The single-scanner test enforces this.
Do not break populate-coderef CLI surface. Flag names, defaults, and exit behavior remain unchanged.
Do not invent a second canonical ID format. createCodeRefId is the single generator.
hard_constraints: No header parser implementation in this phase.
No graph construction or validation changes.
acceptance_criteria: ElementData has optional layer, capability, constraints fields and required headerStatus field.
src/pipeline/element-taxonomy.ts loads the layer enum from STANDARDS/layers.json at runtime.
element-taxonomy.test.ts proves loader correctness, isValidLayer correctness, isKebabCase correctness, and BNF/STANDARDS enum equality.
src/semantic/orchestrator.ts no longer calls astExtractor.extractDirectory(); it consumes PipelineState.
single-scanner.test.ts proves exactly one project-wide AST walk per populate-coderef --semantic run.
populate-coderef --semantic still produces a valid semantic-registry.json (byte-equivalent or with explicitly accepted diff).
Scanner schema docs no longer claim decorators/docstrings/return types/complexity unless those features are actually implemented.
Phase 0 ground-truth tests continue to fail for their original reasons.
All exit-criteria checks (task 1.16) documented in execution notes.

blockers.message: Predecessor workorder artifacts exist, but no matching Phase 0 ground-truth test files were found under __tests__; guard could not be executed from this checkout.

decisions_log.rationale: Roadmap Phase 1 is one of nine sequential gated phases.

decisions_log.decision: layer / capability / constraints are optional; headerStatus is required (default 'missing').
decisions_log.rationale: Phase 1 ships before the header parser. A required layer field would mark every element invalid until Phase 2.5.

decisions_log.decision: Collapse the orchestrator seam, not the projection seam.
decisions_log.rationale: WO-UNIFY-CODEREF-SEMANTIC-SYSTEMS-001 already unified projections. The remaining duplication is in src/semantic/orchestrator.ts.

phase_transitions.reason: Phase 1 implementation and targeted verification completed; hard stop before Phase 2 observed.

next_action: Review Phase 1 execution notes and decide whether to close or separately execute the Phase 0 predecessor tests.

communication_log.message: Workorder created for roadmap Phase 1 (Scanner Identity & Taxonomy Ratification). Phased plan, 17 tasks, hard-stop before Phase 2.

Phase 1 implementation completed and documented in execution-notes.md. Verification passed for tsc, build, targeted vitest, and populate minimal. Phase 0 guard was not runnable because no predecessor test files were present under __tests__.

Workorder closed via /close-workorder --force-warning-ok per DISPATCH-2026-05-02-007. Phase A audit WARNING (cultural-validator 2 warn-severity, tracking-validator 15/15 PASS). Phase 1 deliverable in commit 382e9bb. Phase 0 ground-truth tests now exist (commit 1d1d830) and continue to fail for original reasons — guard re-runnable by future agent.
